title: Cruciform-hinged conjugated polymers unlock the conductivity-hydrophilicity-insolubility triad for high-performance aqueous sodium-ion batteries
authors: Jiang Zhang, Qifeng Jiang, Qian Cheng, Ruoxi Wang, Richen Jia, Changyi Liu, Yuanyuan Xiong, Weina Han, Zexin Jin, Colin Nuckolls, Lan Jiang
abstract: Organic materials for batteries often struggle to be conductive, water compatible, and stable all at once. We designed a conjugated polymer with a twist—the incorporation of “cruciform hinges”—to finally achieve this trifecta. This molecular architecture enables high-power, long-lasting aqueous sodi…

title: Zn8(CH3COO)8-siloxane cluster-based metal-organic frameworks for inverse propylene/propane separation
authors: Xiao-Jing Xie, Qi-Yun Cao, Min-Yi Zhou, Yong-Liang Huang, Heng Zeng, Weigang Lu, Dan Li
abstract: Xie, Cao, et al. introduce a computationally guided approach for identifying clusters that selectively adsorb C3H8 over C3H6. Among various C3H8-selective MOFs, the Zn8(CH3COO)8-siloxane-cluster-based MOFs stand out for their superior separation potential, faster adsorption kinetics, and robust cycl…

title: Hybrid[4]arene-based co-crystals: Perfect vapochromic selectivity and efficient photothermal conversion
authors: Jingyu Chen, Minghao Liang, Haoze Zhu, Yuyue Chi, Wenzhi Yang, Hongyi He, Bo Li, Jiong Zhou
abstract: This work introduces a novel approach to creating efficient photothermal materials using organic co-crystals. The key finding is that co-crystals can be dynamically tuned by absorbing vapor molecules, which alter their charge-transfer properties to achieve high photothermal conversion efficiency. Th…

title: Removing passivation irregularities for seamless electroplating across lithium, zinc, and magnesium metal batteries
authors: Minsung Baek, Jinyoung Kim, Minkwan Kim, Suhwan Kim, Seonmo Yang, Hyo Chul Ahn, Jimin Lee, Kiho Park, Jiheon Kim, Taeyong Lee, Insu Hwang, Nohjoon Lee, Areum Ha, Seongmin Ha, Jongchan Song, Dong-Ik Kim, Heejin Kim, Yong Min Lee, Ki Jae Kim, Jang Wook Choi
abstract: Passivation irregularities and nascent solid-electrolyte interphase layers on metal anodes govern nucleation and growth in lithium, zinc, and magnesium batteries. By eliminating native oxides and constructing uniform infant layers, we realize seamless, compact electrodeposition, establishing passiva…

title: Targeted spin regulation in multi-spin systems via σ-bond rotation
authors: Wei Deng, Shan-Nan Du, Yan-Cong Chen, Jun-Liang Liu, Xiao-Ming Chen, Ming-Liang Tong
abstract: Bond-rotation-assisted coordination-induced spin-state switching (BR-CISSS) in 3d-4f metallacrown molecules is achieved via a non-invasive single-crystal-to-single-crystal technique. This approach offers exceptional reversibility, bidirectional control, clear structure-property relationships, and su…

title: Unlocking the potential of diarylmethane dyes for living systems
authors: Jian Wang, Yang-Yi Ren, Ye-Ying Huang, Zhi-Fan Qian, Gui-Xue Tang, Zhi-Shu Huang, Shuo-Bin Chen, Lin Huang, Jia-Heng Tan
abstract: Many historically known dye scaffolds remain underexplored due to intrinsic chemical limitations. Through rational structural modification of diarylmethane dyes, this study uncovers their hidden bioanalytical potential and yields TiCa, a miniature far-red fluorogen. TiCa selectively recognizes RNA-D…

title: Triplet-triplet annihilation upconversion in covalent organic frameworks via interface and bulk exciton tunability
authors: Ming-Yu Zhang, Lin-Han Jiang, Jia-Yao Li, Hong-Juan Feng, Zhi Huang, Ai-Xin Ma, Dong-Xue Guo, Ling Huang, Dai-Wen Pang
abstract: The bulky substituents on the cyanine sensitizer not only enhance its triplet-state properties but also act as anchoring units, facilitating binding to the surface of an energy-level-matched, annihilator-incorporated COF. This strengthened interfacial connection promotes triplet exciton transfer, fo…

title: Supradynamics: Motion in cavitand complexes
authors: Ya Gao, Pablo Ballester, Gantulga Norjmaa, Fahmi Himo, Julius Rebek, Yang Yu
abstract: Molecular recognition is a dynamic dance of coupled motions. Within cavitands, guests and hosts adapt mutually through spinning, breathing, and folding—a fluid interplay sustained by weak forces on a shallow energy landscape. This paradigm moves beyond static fits to bridge synthetic and biological…

title: Aqueous-phase organic photochemistry: Distinct modes and interfaces
authors: Xia-Min Jiang, Burkhard König, Ya-Ming Tian
abstract: This perspective presents recent advances in aqueous-phase organic photochemistry, highlighting how water serves as an active reaction component through hydrogen bonding, hydrophobic effects, and interfacial electric fields. It systematically compares in-water and on-water reaction paradigms, showca…

title: Zeolite-based catalysts for methane conversion: Challenges and opportunities
authors: Feng Li, Yuan Fang, Yaqi Lai, Liang Wang, Xiangju Meng, Feng-Shou Xiao
abstract: This perspective briefly summarizes recent results from methane conversions, including methane dehydroaromatization, dry reforming of methane, the partial oxidation of methane, the complete oxidation of methane, and the selective catalytic reduction of NOx. It provides a few examples of efficient me…

title: Ultracapacitive bioactive-ion pumps
authors: Panlong Li, Joanna Feder-Kubis, Jonas Kunigkeit, Ashour A. Ahemd, Aleksandra Gersz, Sebastian G. Mucha, Adam Slesinski, Hanfeng Zhou, Przemyslaw Galek, Eike Brunner, Thomas D. Kühne, Julia Grothe, Stefan Kaskel
abstract: The detection of ion dynamics in carbon nanopores and bulk solutions is achieved through the molecular integration of UV-absorbing cations and anions with distinct signatures, alongside in situ spectroscopic techniques. This innovation provides a new understanding of the process by which ions enter…

title: The third wave of enzyme engineering: Navigating the AI-powered IDEA paradigm
authors: Shuaiqi Meng, Shiwei Cheng, Xiao Cheng, Dongzhi Wei, Xiujuan Li, Haiyang Cui
abstract: Enzyme engineering has evolved from random directed evolution to knowledge-guided rational design and is now entering an artificial intelligence (AI)-driven third wave. To capture AI’s transformative impact, we propose the investigate-design-execute-analyze (IDEA) framework, which illustrates how AI…

title: Predict the ceiling
abstract: Ceiling temperature represents a critical parameter for designing recyclable polymers that support a circular economy and mitigate plastic pollution. In the April issue of Cell Reports Sustainability, Broadbelt and co-workers report a machine-learning framework for predicting ceiling temperatures, p…

title: Expanding the territory to post-assembly modification of metallacages
abstract: The functionalization of metallacages represents a pivotal prerequisite for unlocking their potential in biomedical applications. Now, Moreno-Alcántar, Casini, and co-workers report in Cell Reports Physical Science a comprehensive strategy enabling the efficient post-assembly modification (PAM) of P…

title: One step further to efficient Li-mediated ammonia synthesis in flow cells
authors: Ying Sun, Haijun Xu, Jieshan Qiu
abstract: In the February 12 issue of Science, Li and co-workers report a strategy for maintaining sufficient lithium supply at high current densities in Li-mediated ammonia synthesis by tuning the solid-electrolyte interphase. Their method achieves a landmark Faradaic efficiency of 98% with an outstanding en…

title: Orchestrating the bone-healing cascade: A multifunctional 3D scaffold with synergistic biochemical and photothermal modules
authors: Chenyu Deng, Tingting Yu, Bing Han
abstract: Achieving effective bone regeneration demands both spatiotemporal precision and dynamic control over multiple biological phases. In the March issue of Cell Press Blue, Li et al. present a therapeutic platform that integrates physical stimulation with tailored scaffold design and biochemical cues to…

title: Learning catalytic patterns from stratified synthetic data
abstract: In a recent Chem Catalysis article, Liao, Nguyen, and Liu infer governing laws in heterogeneous catalysis by applying machine-learning models to kinetic Monte Carlo simulations of methanol synthesis on copper. Stratifying the resulting synthetic data by activity and selectivity enables identificatio…

title: Steering cobalt phase evolution for efficient syngas-to-light-olefin conversion
authors: Zeyan Cen, Tiancheng Pu, Xing-Wu Liu, Ding Ma
abstract: In a recent issue of Nature, Han et al. show that physically mixing hydroxyl-containing promoters with a Co2MnO4 precursor redirects catalyst evolution toward a cooperative anorthic Co–Mn oxide/carbide working state; at 250°C–260°C and 0.1 MPa, this system achieves 70%–82% CO conversion with >60% li…

title: Hierarchical meso-supraparticles for energy storage and conversion
authors: Guangmei Jiang, Yujuan Zhao, Meng Kong, Dongliang Chao, Zhenyuan Teng, Dongyuan Zhao, Zaiwang Zhao
abstract: Hierarchical meso-supraparticles with multi-level structures can integrate macro-, meso-, and micropores, featuring well-defined spatial arrangements and topological connectivity, thereby demonstrating great application in energy storage and conversion. This article reviews the synthesis strategies…
